race.name: Oregon Gran Fondo
race.tagline: All-road cycling through western Oregon forests, vineyards, and historic landscapes - Round #5 of the Oregon Triple Crown Series

vitals.registration: Open with early pricing through Jan 2, 2026. Under-23 riders: 40% off with code 'Youngcyclist26'. Series pass discounts up to 25%. Register at bikereg.com/oregon-gran-fondo
vitals.prize_purse: Awards for 11 divisions; top finishers receive free/50% off next-year entry
vitals.aid_stations: Mile 33 (Wolf Creek Rd), mile 53 (Esmond Creek/Siuslaw turnaround), final aid ~mile 94 after timing chip (6 miles from finish)
vitals.cutoff_time: Hard cutoff at mile 33 aid station: 11:30 AM
vitals.entry_fee: Early bird pricing available through Jan 2, 2026 (exact fees not specified)

climate.overview: Mid-May in Cottage Grove: typically mild 50-70°F (10-21°C), possible rain

terrain.overview: All-road paved routes through western Oregon forests, vineyards, historic landscapes, and quiet backroads. Marked with 5-mile markers, yard signs, pink arrows. GPX files via Ride with GPS.

logistics.explanation: The Oregon Gran Fondo offers smooth logistics with a parade start from Bohemia Park through downtown Cottage Grove, multiple well-stocked aid stations like Aid Alpha at mile 33 with hard cutoffs, and an early start option, though minor issues like the final 6-mile non-timed return slightly detract from perfection.[1][2]

length.explanation: The event provides excellent length options of 100-mile Gran and 69-mile Medio routes, perfectly embodying the 'Big Ride' Gran Fondo tradition with substantial distances for endurance enthusiasts.[1][2]

technicality.explanation: The all-road challenge through western forests features moderate technical demands with some difficult terrain and steeper hills, but remains accessible without extreme gravel or singletrack sections.[1][6][7]

elevation.explanation: Significant elevation gain across the 100-mile loop through forested landscapes and historic areas delivers a challenging climb profile, earning high marks for vertical adventure.[1][7]

climate.explanation: Held in mid-May in Cottage Grove, the climate typically offers mild spring weather ideal for cycling through vineyards and forests, minimizing risks of extreme heat or cold.[2][4]

altitude.explanation: Starting and finishing near sea level in Cottage Grove with low-lying routes through valleys and creeks, the event lacks high-altitude challenges.[1][2]

adventure.explanation: Riders experience thrilling adventure via all-road loops through western forests, vineyards, historic landscapes, and a no-racing turnaround zone, creating an exploratory Gran Fondo vibe.[1][6]

prestige.explanation: As part of Oregon’s Triple Crown races alongside events like Sasquatch Duro, it holds regional prestige but lacks broader national or international acclaim.[1]

race_quality.explanation: High-quality organization by Mudslinger Events includes chip timing, ample aid with Hammer Heed and PB&J, porta potties at stations, and dry camping, ensuring a professional mass-start experience.[2]

experience.explanation: The inclusive mass-start from Bohemia Park unites pros and everyday cyclists in a celebratory ride through stunning Oregon scenery, delivering an unforgettable community-focused Gran Fondo.[1][4]

community.explanation: Strong community emphasis shines through the parade start, local involvement in Cottage Grove, and invitation to all ages/abilities, fostering a true cycling celebration.[1][2][4]

field_depth.explanation: Limited to 500 riders with mixed abilities from pros to recreational cyclists, the field lacks deep elite competition depth typical of larger pro events.[4][7]

value.explanation: Excellent value from comprehensive aid, camping options, and epic routes through forests and vineyards at a community-oriented price point makes it a standout for the cost.[2]

expenses.explanation: Reasonable expenses with dry camping at Bohemia Park, local lodging in Cottage Grove, and no excessive fees enhance affordability for this high-quality Gran Fondo.[2]

ratings_explanations.logistics: Well-supported with aid stations, clear route marking, GPX files, and Eugene Airport 20-30 min away[1][2][3]
ratings_explanations.length: Gran 100mi, Medio 69mi - true gran fondo distances[1][2]
ratings_explanations.technicality: High-speed sharp turns (Territorial Rd), but primarily paved roads[2]
ratings_explanations.elevation: Reported 9,000ft total climbing including Wolf Creek Rd[2]
ratings_explanations.climate: Mild spring weather, low altitude, possible PNW rain[2][3]
ratings_explanations.altitude: Low elevation western Oregon - no high-altitude suffering[2]
ratings_explanations.adventure: Forests, vineyards, historic landscapes through beautiful Oregon backcountry[1][6]
ratings_explanations.prestige: Top 10 North American gran fondo, Triple Crown series event[2][7]
ratings_explanations.race_quality: Timed, supported, awards in 11 divisions with competitive series scoring[2][5]
ratings_explanations.experience: Parade start through downtown, community festival atmosphere, pro/everyday mix[1][3]
ratings_explanations.community: Strong Oregon cycling community focus, welcomes all abilities[1][2][5]
ratings_explanations.field_depth: Intimate field but includes competitive series riders[2]
ratings_explanations.value: Series discounts, youth pricing, jersey option - great value[2][5]
ratings_explanations.expenses: Reasonable entry with early pricing and travel costs from EUG[2][3]

biased_opinion.overview: The Oregon Gran Fondo perfectly captures the true gran fondo spirit - everyday cyclists rolling out with the pros on a parade start through beautiful Oregon countryside. As round #5 of the Triple Crown Series, it offers legitimate 100mi distance with 9,000ft climbing on quiet paved roads through forests and vineyards. Intimate, community-focused, and incredibly scenic - this is cycling as it should be.

final_verdict.one_liner: T2 franchise event - authentic gran fondo experience with strong community and series prestige

logistics.overview: Eugene Airport (EUG) 20-30min north. Start/finish Bohemia Park, Cottage Grove with parade through downtown. Dry camping available. Registration via bikereg.com. Early pricing through Jan 2, 2026[1][2][3]

history.overview: Founded 2013 (14th edition 2026). Community-focused gran fondo inspired by Italian/French traditions where everyday cyclists ride with pros. Part of Oregon Triple Crown Series[1][3][6]

course_description.character: 100mi Gran Fondo: Aid mile 33 Wolf Creek Rd (11:30am cutoff), mile 53 Esmond Creek/Siuslaw turnaround (no-racing zone), final aid ~mile 94 after timing stops 6mi from Cottage Grove. Features high-speed sharp turns on Territorial Rd after Wolf Creek descent. 69mi Medio Fondo similar early sections. **Suffering Zones**: Wolf Creek Rd climb (mile 33 cutoff), Esmond Creek remote turnaround[2]

non_negotiables: Download GPX file from Ride with GPS
Note timing stops 6 miles from finish
No route changes after packet pickup
Early start 8:00am available for traditional bikes
